div.h3: Memory Protection & Privilege Levels (Ring 0 vs Ring 3)

ul.li: Context Switching: Each process has a unique CR3 register value pointing to its private Page Directory.
Shared Kernel Space: Entries 768-1023 are identical across all directories to allow kernel services, but protected by the U/S (User/Supervisor) bit.
Privilege Level (CPL): The CPU checks the CPL against the page's U/S bit. If CPL=3 and U/S=0, the MMU generates an exception.
Memory Safety: Process A cannot even "see" Process B's memory because it is not mapped in PD_A.
